rulings.Case Title: COLBY LAM VS. ANDREW KENNY ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANTs M-J SF INVESTMENTS, LLC, MICHAEL CASSIDY, BRIDGET HARTY-CASSIDY, THE BANNER DEVELOPMENT COMPANY's DEMURRER TO 2ND AMENDED COMPLAINT SUSTAINED in part and OVERRULED in part. Plaintiffs' causes of action, as alleged contain two distinct causes of action in each. Specifically cause of action arising out of injuries to person and cause of action arising out of injuries to property. Allstate Ins. Co. v. Mel Rapton, Inc. (2000) 77 Cal.App.4th 901. Demurrer to each cause of action, to the extent it arises out of injury to property, is sustained without leave to amend: Plaintiff states in opposition to the demurrer that Plaintiff "seeks only damages for personal injuries." Demurrer to all causes of action, to the extent they arise out of personal injury, is OVERRULED. Plaintiff sufficiently alleges delayed discovery. Contrary to Defendant's reading of the allegations in the SAC, Plaintiff never alleges that he, as opposed to others, knew about the defects in February 2016 or at any time before January 2018. Demurrer to alter ego allegations is sustained with final leave to amend. The Court has previously instructed Plaintiff to allege facts, not conclusions. Plaintiff chose to not make any substantive amendments to alter ego allegations. to allege facts giving rise to conclusory statements in SAC, e.g. facts giving rise to the conclusion that Defendants "failed to observe normal corporate and business formalities" or "failed to keep proper accounting records," etc. = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: COLBY LAM VS. ANDREW KENNY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion To Quash Or Modify Plaintiff'S Subpoena Seeking Financial Records From First-Citizens Bank And Trust Company, Including Request For Monetary Sanctions
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2018 line 2. DEFENDANTs M-J SF INVESTMENTS, LLC, JAMES NUNEMACHER, THE BANNER DEVELOPMENT COMPANY, A CALIFORNIA, MICHAEL CASSIDY, and BRIDGET HARTY-CASSIDY's Motion To Quash Or Modify Plaintiff'S Subpoena Seeking Financial Records From First-Citizens Bank And Trust Company, Including Request For Monetary Sanctions denied in part granted in part. The subpoena will not be quashed. The requested material is calculated to lead to the discovery of admissible relevant evidence; discovery is broad unless not designed to lead to discovery of admissible relevant evidence, or privileged. The subpoena is hereby modified to exclude personal financial information that was submitted to obtain the loan or financing, based on the moving parties' privacy rights. Defendants do not dispute plaintiff's argument that entities have limited or no privacy rights. The Court rejects defendants' request that they serve as gatekeeper on the requested documents, given their conflict of interest. Sanctions are awarded against moving parties and their counsel of record in the amount of $6,375 because defendants failed to engage in good faith meet and confer and misrepresented the subpoena to the Court in its papers by omitting the important capitalized portion of the subpoena that explicitly states, "NO FINANCIAL INFORMATION OF THE BORROWER(S) IS REQUESTED BY THIS SUBPOENA." = (501/REQ)

rulings.Case Title: DEUTSCHE BANK NATIONAL TRUST COMPANY VS. BEATRICE GISIE ET AL
rulings.Rulings: Real Property/Housing Court Law and Motion Calendar for October 31, 2018 line 7. DEFENDANT BEATRICE GISIE's DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT OVERRULED, 5 days to answer. Complaint sufficiently alleges facts to support a cause of action for unlawful detainer under CCP 1161a. Defendant improperly raises factual issues beyond the scope of demurrer and some of those issues even go beyond the scope of this UD action altogether. Plaintiff's late-filed and improperly served opposition is stricken. Opposition was due on October 18, 2018. Plaintiff served its opposition on October 24, 2018 (reply due date) and served it by mail in violation of CCP 1005 and CCP 1013. Opposition provides no explanation was these violations. Additionally, Plaintiff failed to comply with SFRL 2.7B. Plaintiff and its counsel is ordered to comply with all procedural requirements per CCP, CRC, SFLR, etc. in future. = (501/REQ)
